decks.notes: This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays and disruption spells to control the board and overwhelm the opponent. By playing efficient creatures like Amped Raptor and Guide of Souls, along with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Cabal Therapy, the deck aims to apply constant pressure while disrupting the opponent's game plan. The inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provides additional ways to close out the game quickly. The mana base, including lands like Badlands and Karakas, ensures the deck can cast its spells consistently and efficiently.

The main concept of this UR Tempo aggro deck is to control the opponent's resources through disruption spells like Force of Will and Daze while applying pressure with efficient creatures like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler. The deck aims to establish an early aggressive presence on the battlefield while disrupting the opponent's game plan, utilizing cards like Wasteland and Lightning Bolt to further hinder their strategy. By efficiently using countermagic and tempo plays, this deck aims to maintain a strong board presence and close out the game quickly before the opponent can recover.

This deck focuses on utilizing disruptive spells and removal to maintain control over the game while deploying threats that can quickly close out the game. The main strategy is to disrupt the opponent's plans using cards like Daze and Force of Will, while deploying efficient creatures like Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters to pressure the opponent. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and utility, while cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push help to deal with opposing threats. Overall, this deck aims to maintain tempo by efficiently dealing with threats and applying pressure to the opponent.

This deck focuses on quickly dropping powerful Eldrazi creatures onto the battlefield to overwhelm the opponent. Utilizing cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Glaring Fleshraker, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining pressure with aggressive creatures. The deck also includes plenty of mana acceleration in the form of Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide, allowing for quick deployment of key threats. With disruption from Chalice of the Void and control elements like Kozilek's Command, this deck aims to finish the game swiftly before the opponent can stabilize.

This control deck focuses on using counterspells and removal spells to disrupt opponents while gradually gaining card advantage with cards like Brainstorm and Stock Up. The deck also utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler to control the board and protect itself. It aims to control the game by countering threats and removing problematic permanents, while also having access to card draw and recursion with Mystic Sanctuary and Uro, Titan of Nature's Wrath. The deck combines efficient removal spells with powerful spells to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents in a 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on utilizing powerful creatures and disruptive spells to quickly apply pressure on opponents while also controlling the board. With a mix of aggressive creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Skyclave Apparition, along with disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while beating them down. The inclusion of Aether Vial allows for quick creature deployment, while cards like Stoneforge Mystic and Recruiter of the Guard provide additional utility and resilience. The deck also includes a Companion, Yorion, Sky Nomad, to provide additional value and versatility.

This deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with efficient creatures. By using cards like Brainstorm, Thoughtseize, and Force of Will to control the board and protect valuable threats like Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf, this deck seeks to out tempo the opponent and win the game quickly. With a solid mana base and a mix of removal spells like Fatal Push and Wasteland, the Dimir Aggro Tempo deck aims to maintain control of the game while putting the opponent on the back foot.

The main concept of this combo Mystic Forge deck is to quickly ramp up mana with cards like Grim Monolith and Urza's Tower to cast powerful artifacts like The One Ring and Planar Nexus. By utilizing cards like Manifold Key and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain, the deck aims to generate infinite combos and overwhelm opponents with powerful artifacts. Support cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional utility and board control. The goal is to create a dominant board presence and win the game through overwhelming artifact synergy and combo potential.

This deck aims to quickly mill through the entire library, using cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to put all the cards in the graveyard. Thassa's Oracle is then used to win the game instantly by having no cards left in the library. The deck also includes protection spells like Pact of Negation and Cabal Therapy to ensure the combo goes off without interruption. The addition of cards like Simian Spirit Guide and Chrome Mox helps to ramp up mana quickly, allowing for a faster combo execution.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly generate mana and power up artifact creatures to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. By utilizing cards like Mox Opal, Lotus Petal, and Urza's Saga, the deck can quickly create a board presence with creatures like Patchwork Automaton and Memory Guardian. The deck also includes disruption spells like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor to control the opponent's plays, while also utilizing Emry, Lurker of the Loch for card advantage. Overall, this deck combines artifact synergy with aggressive plays to win the game quickly.

The deck focuses on quickly comboing off using a combination of free mana sources, discard spells, and tutors to dump your entire library into your graveyard. This leads to a win condition using Thassa's Oracle or another combo piece to win the game on the spot. The key strategy is to carefully navigate through disruption and protect your combo with counterspells while efficiently executing your game plan. The deck prioritizes speed and consistency, aiming to win the game as quickly as possible before the opponent can establish their own game plan.

The deck focuses on quickly playing powerful creatures while disrupting the opponent's game plan. It aims to take control of the game early by dropping threats like Chancellor of the Annex and Magus of the Moon, while using cards like Trinisphere to slow down the opponent. The deck also utilizes fast mana sources like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide to accelerate its game plan. Overall, it aims to assert dominance on the board early and maintain pressure throughout the game.

The deck focuses on generating a high amount of mana quickly and using that mana to chain together multiple spells in a single turn to create a storm count, eventually leading to a powerful finisher like Tendrils of Agony. It aims to draw cards, tutor for key combo pieces, and disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Gamble, Silence, and Hexing Squelcher. The deck is designed to be fast and resilient, taking advantage of efficient mana accelerants like Lotus Petal and Lion's Eye Diamond to win the game before the opponent can establish their own game plan.

The key concept of this Dragon Stompy deck is to disrupt the opponent's mana base and control the board with powerful creatures while using aggressive tactics to quickly overwhelm them. By utilizing cards like Blood Moon, Magus of the Moon, and Chalice of the Void to restrict the opponent's options, as well as fast mana acceleration with Simian Spirit Guide and Chrome Mox, the deck aims to establish a dominant position early in the game. The deck's finishers, such as Fury, Pyrogoyf, and Sundering Eruption, provide the final blow to secure victory.

This deck aims to quickly get out powerful creatures such as Emrakul and Atraxa by using Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. It includes a lot of card draw and control elements to ensure the combo pieces come together smoothly. The inclusion of Boseiju and Veil of Summer help protect key spells from counterspells. The deck also features some land acceleration with Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb to help ramp into the big creatures. Overall, the strategy revolves around cheating out game-changing creatures early in the game to overwhelm the opponent.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on aggressively controlling the board while maintaining card advantage. Utilizing cards like Fatal Push, Thoughtseize, and Force of Will to disrupt the opponent's game plan, it also includes threats like Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf to apply pressure. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card selection and advantage, while cards like Reanimate can bring back powerful creatures from the graveyard. The deck's mana base is optimized with fetch lands and dual lands, along with disruption tools like Daze and Wasteland to keep the opponent off balance.
